name: hDNAgr therapy
summary: Fragmented double-stranded DNA therapy proposed for hematopoietic stem-cell genome repair and telomere effects, currently represented only by a not-yet-recruiting no-results aging registry.
mechanism_summary: The registry describes fragmented therapeutic double-stranded DNA intended to correct or replace damaged genomic regions in hematopoietic stem cells and elongate telomeres; this mechanism remains unproven in treated-human outcome data.

open_questions: Whether the registry begins enrollment and posts interpretable safety data
Whether telomere, CD34-positive progenitor, epigenetic-age, or SF-36 endpoints move versus placebo
Whether the proposed genome-reconstruction mechanism can be verified without unacceptable proliferative, genomic, or immune risk
